prompt: What does `SortGroup().set_group_by_electrode_group(nwb_file_name=nwb_file)` actually do — what defines a sort group when you call it? And on a multi-shank probe, why does this conflict with the clusterless decoding / spike sorting pipeline?
behavioral_checks: Identifies that set_group_by_electrode_group groups by electrode_group_name (one sort group per electrode_group)
Explains the clusterless failure: multi-shank electrode_groups produce duplicate (x, y) contact positions; SpikeInterface rejects at SpikeSortingRecording.populate()
Recommends set_group_by_shank for clusterless / waveform-based pipelines
Notes that set_group_by_electrode_group is only available in the v0 API (spyglass.spikesorting.v0), not in v1

Does not claim set_group_by_electrode_group produces one electrode per sort group (it groups by electrode_group_name, which can include multiple electrodes per group — that is precisely the source of the multi-shank conflict)
